Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Hold on to your sewing chairs! The Midnight Quilt Show is going bold, as in late-night foundation paper-piecing bold. (Angela makes it easy!)
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: George Vondriska and Matt Newborg work together to share some of their best sanding techniques that are guaranteed to save you time in the shop. Find out which sanding techniques work best with different types of wood, learn the difference between various types of sandpaper, and see which tools are available for your sanding needs.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Choosing the Right Sanding Method

Video.summary: Make technology your friend. It's hard to keep up with each and every new trend but you can find your favorites and use them to your advantage. We begin with an awesome page, then it's a project life page with Nancy Nally using instagram photos, and finally a family heirloom with modern techniques but historic info from Paula Nelson-Hart. Great for young family members to get to know their heritage and for older family members to remember and reflect.
Video.title: Keeping Up

Video.summary: Timing is everything! Doing steps in the right order, waiting for a resin to set, letting glue dry -- these are issues we are all aware of. In this episode we look at projects where timing makes the difference. First is a foiling project, where order makes the difference. Then Dina Wakley has a paint technique using rubbing alcohol. Finally, Joe Rotella makes a wine cork shadow box personalized with a favorite sentiment.
Video.title: It's All in the Timing

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Today's experts provide a new perspective on machine attachments. You can do so many techniques faster and easier when you choose the right accessory. Cookie Gaynor is first with an updated take on circles using a circle attachment and decorative stitches. Then, Angela Wolf's Little Black Dress lesson is hemming the vent on your dress. Finally, Clare Rowley shares her expertise on using sewing feet for easy ways to attach, trim, pearls, and sequins for an updated basic tee shirt.
Video.title: Classic Machine Attachments

Video.summary: Express your style in metalwork and with lettering, too. Mary Hettmansperger adds a window in metal work to make a versatile bezel. She adds style by layering metals and putting a variety of motifs behind the window. Creative collaborator Brenda Schweder shares her favorite tips on soldering metalwork. Katie Hacker puts her style into words with lettering in Katie's Beading Lesson.
Video.title: Metalworking in Style

Video.summary: In the 1980's, designers took their cues from active wear, workout clothes and easy care fabrics. Angela Wolf adds a sassy peplum to a basic t-shirt to add swing and style. Cindy Hogan uses the latest digital cutting technology to create appliques for a go-everywhere denim jacket.
Video.title: Fashion on the Go - the 1980's

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Bring the fun to any gathering and impress your guests with handmade wooden games you can make right in the shop. George Vondriska shows you how to make a game board for chess and checkers, as well as a brain-teasing ring stacking game to challenge your friends.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Chess/Checker Board & Ring Stacking Games

Video.summary: Scraps and pieces become beautiful quilts. Scrappy appliquĂ© artist Shannon Brinkley collages scraps of fabric into whimsical quilted designs. Jacquie Gering and Heather Grant discuss guild scholarship programs. Using small scraps, author and paper piecing expert Elizabeth Dackson shows techniques for foundation paper piecing.
Video.title: Scraps and Pieces

Video.summary: Believe it or not, this quilt is only made of straight seams! Watch it "unfold" as Angela conquers the Strip City pattern with Boundless blues.
Video.title: Strip City

Video.summary: Angela brings you her laurel wreath quilt, the perfect excuse to showcase your most prized scraps and highlight it all with gorgeous free-motion quilting.
Video.title: Laurel Wreath

Video.summary: Angela's been challenged by The Crafty Gemini to make a stash-busting tote bag. So she's stepping outside her comfort zone and up to the #MidnightStashChallenge!
Video.title: Quilted Tote Bag Challenge

Video.summary: Angela’s not the only Midnight Quilter in the Walters' house! Watch as she and her teenage daughter Cloe spend some quality time together making an easy-to-piece charm quilt.
Video.title: Like a Charm

Video.summary: This week Angela is preparing to host a dinner for quilters, so of course, she has to quilt up a few placemats!
Video.title: Quilt-As-You-Go Placemats

Video.summary: The holidays are coming, but don't stress! We teamed up with Angela Walters, host of the Midnight Quilt Show, to create three DIY quilt projects that are super gift-able. In this episode, you'll learn how to make a cathedral window pillow, an adorable gift for someone in your life who you've deemed "quilt-worthy." - P.S. Don't miss Angela's tips for non-quilters on what to do (and what NOT to do!) when you receive a gift from a quilter...
Video.title: Cathedral Window Pillow

Video.summary: Picking out your first quilting pattern can feel a bit overwhelming — but Angela is here to help! Learn how big blocks, straight lines and no matching points can make all the difference for beginner quilters.
Video.title: Bonus: Easy Quilt Patterns for Beginners

Image.alt: Bonus: Easy Quilt Patterns for Beginners
Bonus: Easy Quilt Patterns for Beginners
Bonus: Easy Quilt Patterns for Beginners

Video.summary: The Walters household is eating healthy this week, but that doesn't mean Angela's quilts have to be. Today she is whipping up the Milky Way Quilt by Monique Dillard featuring Lily & Loom fabric.
Video.title: Milky Way

Video.summary: In the Season 5 finale of The Midnight Quilt show, Angela is creating a modern quilt of her own design that involves a 36-patch block!
Video.title: Modern X

Video.summary: Angela shows you how to easily make a stash-buster pieced quilt back that can either match or contrast with your quilt top.
Video.title: Stash Busting Pieced Quilt Back

Video.summary: CAUTION: Baby quilts are a total gateway drug! Just ask Angela: she's working this cute little number up for her newborn nephew. (Think you can stop at one? Don't say we didn't warn you!)
Video.title: Z Is for Zoo

Video.summary: Look to nature for design inspiration. Using quartz points, designer Molly Schaller creates stunning necklaces and other jewelry. To work out, she makes jewelry from pearls in all shapes, colors and sizes. Artist Jill MacKay cools down with designs in die-cut leather. Katie Hacker discusses how bead-making can benefit children facing health challenges.
Video.title: Work Out Naturally

Video.summary: Author Laura Poplin combines the ancient art of leatherwork and chainmaille to make beautiful contemporary jewelry. Learn to make colorful dyed and painted leather rectangles and finish them into jewelry using classic leatherworking techniques. Laura shows how to connect leather pieces with Byzantine chainmaile weave to make a cuff style bracelet. Katie demonstrates a quick and easy cup chain bracelet.
Video.title: Leather and Chainmaille Jewelry

Video.summary: Make your own special occasion with a unique card. First, Jenn Mason creates a photo card. Next, May Flaum uses a stamped image for her card design. Host Julie Fei-Fan Balzer interviews Kazan Clark. Then, Julie shows how to create a card background using photo corners. Last, Julie creates a card with a house shaped cut out.
Video.title: Episode 1: Cards

Video.summary: Add a different tool, a new surface, or just make a subtle change to shape up your techniques. First, host Julie Fei Fan Balzer creates a handmade journal cover using watercolor powders. Next, Micah Goguen shares a technique for creating frosted vintage botanicals. Then, Sandy McTier has a few tricks for decorative painting with her festive pumpkins project. Last is a color study -- the color grey.
Video.title: Episode 4: Your Techniques

Video.summary: Hold onto your needles! Tracie and Jodi, are here to dish out fresh fiber inspiration. From project picks to wooly good gossip, things are about to get super knitty… and maybe just a little nutty.
Video.title: Episode 13

Video.summary: Give your knitting the happy ending it deserves! In the season finale of Off Our Needles, Tracie and Jodi talk about the bind-offs they love and the projects they never wanted to end.
Video.title: Knitting So Good You Never Want to End + Favorite Bind-Offs

Image.alt: Knitting So Good You Never Want to End + Favorite Bind-Offs
Knitting So Good You Never Want to End + Favorite Bind-Offs
Knitting So Good You Never Want to End + Favorite Bind-Offs

Video.summary: Baffled by brioche? Don't be! Tune in to see Lesley Anne Robinson and the Grocery Girls tackle this super squishy, double-sided fabric.
Video.title: The Wonderful World of BRIOCHE Knitting

Image.alt: The Wonderful World of BRIOCHE Knitting
The Wonderful World of BRIOCHE Knitting
The Wonderful World of BRIOCHE Knitting

Video.summary: Since when do knitters use crochet hooks? Find out how to work the provisional cast-on, cable cast-on, and more during the first Novel-T check in!
Video.title: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline

Image.alt: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline

Video.summary: Use what you make and get double fun from your crafts. Weave hip survival bracelets with some tips from Katie. Franz uses everyday objects to make a robot storage container. Blogger Rachel Howard and her kids make a miniature construction site for toy earth-moving equipment! Use items from the Family Craft Box and recycle old road maps to craft a travel case full of learning games and activities -- and treats, too!
Video.title: Use It

Video.summary: Today's word of the day is distortion. This is when we take one aspect of something and change it or make it different -- usually not the normal way we might look at something. Our first examples are abstract portraits using magazines. Then, create a fun house mirror using tiles. Our art journal technique uses plastic wrap with water colors to create unusual distorted backgrounds. Last, use reverse shape cutouts to make unique art works.
Video.title: Distortion

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
